# Invoices & Quotes Features Analysis

## 1\. Introduction

This document outlines the features of Invoices and Quotes modules. It acts as a reference for product development, highlighting standard features, workflow differences, and UI/UX elements.

## 2\. Invoices Module

### 2.1 Invoices List Page

![Invoices List Page][image1]

*Invoices List Page*

* **Navigation:** Accessible via Projects \> Invoices (often a sub-tab).

* **Tabs:** Toggle between Invoices and Quotes.

* **Search & Filter:**

  * **Search:** Text input to search by client name, invoice number, or project.

  * **Sorting:** Sortable columns (Date, Status, Amount, etc.).

* **List Columns:**

  * **Contact:** Client avatar, name, and company.

  * **Invoice \#:** Unique identifier (e.g., 0003).

  * **Project/Lead:** Associated project name and workflow status (e.g., “Venue & equipment rentals”).

  * **Due:** Due date or status (e.g., “No due date”).

  * **Total:** Invoice amount.

  * **Status:** Visual badges (Unpaid, Paid, Overdue, Draft).

* **Row Actions (Context Menu …):** ![Row Actions Menu][image2]

  * Open Invoice

  * Copy Public Link

  * Manage Payments (Record/View)

  * Edit Invoice

  * Send Invoice

  * Recurring Settings (Convert to recurring)

  * Download PDF

  * Duplicate

  * Delete

### 2.2 Create/Edit Invoice Flow

![Create Invoice Page][image3]

*Create Invoice Page*

The editor uses a clean, block-based layout.

#### *Header Section*

* **Customer:** Select existing contact or add new.

* **Dates:** Issue Date (default: today) and Due Date.

* **Invoice \#:** Auto-generated, editable.

* **Options:** ![Header Options][image4]

  * “Bill as Company” toggle.

  * Add PO Number.

  * Add Custom Fields.

#### *Line Items Section*

#### *Line Items Section*

* **Fields:** Item Name, Description, Rate, Quantity, Amount.

* **Tax:** Toggle/Select tax rate per line item or globally. ![Tax Settings][image5] *Developer Note:* The tax modal allows granular control over tax application (inclusive/exclusive) and custom rates.

* **Templates:** Quick-add items from a “Saved Items” or “Packages” list.

#### *Enhancements (Add-ons)*

![Enhancements Menu][image6]

*Enhancements Menu*

* **Description:** Rich text block for memos/notes.

* **Contract:** Attach a digital contract for signature.

* **Attachments:** File upload area.

* **Event:** Link to a calendar event.

#### *Payment Settings*

![Payment Settings Options][image7]

*Payment Settings Options*

#### *Payment Settings (Deep Dive)*

The payment settings are extensive and critical for the workflow.

* **Payment Schedule:** ![Payment Schedule][image8]

  * Options include: Start with deposit, Retainers, Split payments (Milestones).

  * *Developer Note:* Selecting “Recurring” creates a series and changes the available schedule options.

* **Discounts:** ![Discount Options][image9]

  * Supports Percentage (%) or Fixed Amount ($).

* **Gratuity:** ![Gratuity Settings][image10]

  * Client tipping can be enabled/disabled.

* **Payment Methods:** ![Payment Configurations][image11]

  1. Granular toggle for Credit Card vs ACH (Bank Transfer).

  2. Fees can be passed to the client (often a toggle in global settings, but visible here).

#### *Footer / Actions*

* **Live Preview:** Tabs for “Payment Page” (Client view), “Email Preview”, “PDF”. ![Client View Payment Page][image12] *Developer Note:* The client view is a clean, simplified version of the invoice focused on the “Pay” CTA.

* **Actions:** Save Draft, Send, Preview.

---

## 3\. Quotes Module

### 3.1 Quotes List Page

![Quotes List Page][image13]

*Quotes List Page*

* **Structure:** Identical column layout to Invoices but filtered for Quotes.

* **Statuses:** Draft, Sent, Accepted, Expired, Declined.

* **Actions:** Similar row actions (Edit, Send, View, Delete), plus “Convert to Invoice” (often handled via status change).

### 3.2 Create/Edit Quote Flow

![Create Quote Page][image14]

*Create Quote Page*

Visual similarity to Invoice editor but with **Proposal-specific logic**.

#### *Key Differences from Invoices*

* **Expiration Date:** Set a valid-until date for the proposal. ![Expiration Date Picker][image15]

* **Approval Workflow:**

  * Client sees an **“Accept”** button instead of “Pay”.

  * **Signature:** Optional requirement for e-signature upon acceptance. ![Approval Settings][image16]

* **Auto-Conversion:**

  * Settings to automatically **convert to Invoice** upon acceptance.

  * Option to require a deposit immediately upon acceptance (seamless Quote \-\> Invoice \-\> Payment flow).

### 3.3 Quote Preview

* **Accept Page:** Client-facing view to review details and click “Accept”. ![Quote Accept Page][image17]

* **Contract Integration:** If a contract is attached, the flow is “Sign Contract \-\> Accept Quote”.
